mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.8% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, compresses equity valuations, and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.8% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising industrial production and declining real personal savings indicates a supply-constrained demand surge that fuels corporate margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling 2-year OLS regression, or forward returns decouple from the joint signal in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, aggressive federal funds rate hikes fail to cool demand, instead triggering a liquidity crunch that forces equity deleveraging and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns do not decline when both high inflation and rate hikes coincide, or the signal predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX spikes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP directly expands corporate earnings bases, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as fundamental valuation anchors rise.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates show no correlation with subsequent 60-day equity returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or earnings revisions fail to track GDP momentum.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 for two consecutive months while housing starts remain below 1400, it signals a consumption-driven expansion that overheats the services sector, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS > 200k for 2 consecutive months AND HOUST < 1400
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances, or the effect disappears when adjusted for sector-specific earnings shocks.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production stagnation combined with rising unemployment, supply chain bottlenecks resolve while labor slack increases, reducing input costs and boosting equity margins, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0.1% for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward returns remain negative or flat when industrial output stagnates alongside rising unemployment, or the margin expansion hypothesis is contradicted by declining corporate guidance.

mechanism_narrative: A consistent month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals robust economic momentum that supports risk appetite and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as institutional capital rotates into cyclical equities.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no predictive power for 5-day equity returns (Rank IC < 0.01), or risk-on rotation fails to materialize despite strong labor data.
